title: PPG Industries (PPG) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of PPG Industries (PPG) compiled entirely by Gemini.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 4.0% growth rate assumes steady, single-digit growth driven by GDP-plus volume recovery in aerospace and automotive end-markets, combined with the pricing power to offset raw material inflation.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% discount rate reflects PPG's strong balance sheet, geographically diversified operations, and lower risk profile as a Dividend King, while still accounting for cyclical industrial exposure.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate is used. As a mature industrial compounder operating in legacy end-markets, it is reasonable to align long-term growth with global inflation and GDP targets.

thesisParagraphs: PPG Industries represents a classic, high-quality industrial compounder. Operating in an oligopolistic global coatings market, it benefits from high barriers to entry and massive switching costs for its commercial and industrial customers. Once an automotive or aerospace manufacturer qualifies a PPG coating, changing suppliers introduces significant operational and safety risks. This dynamic creates a very sticky, reliable customer base.

Despite being cyclical in nature, its $1.16B in annual free cash flow is robust enough to continuously fund a legendary track record of dividend increases (over 50 years) and share repurchases. Based on a conservative 4% growth rate and a 9% discount rate, the current price of $98.38 appears to offer a compelling margin of safety. The market may be overly discounting near-term macroeconomic weakness in Europe and Asia, ignoring the inherent durability and pricing power of PPG's core business model.
verdictFooter: Based on latest available financial data (March 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini project a 4% FCF growth rate for PPG?
faq.answer: Gemini modeled a conservative 4% growth rate, reflecting the mature nature of the global coatings industry. This assumes modest volume growth combined with consistent pricing actions to offset inflation, driving steady free cash generation.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for PPG's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9% discount rate was used, acknowledging its strong financial position, diversified revenue streams across cyclical and non-cyclical end-markets, and its status as a highly reliable dividend payer.

faq.question: Is it safe to rely on AI for industrial stock valuation?
faq.answer: No. This analysis is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on a specific set of inputs and rigid formulas. It is not financial advice. AI models cannot predict macroeconomic cycles, supply chain disruptions, or shifts in automotive production.
